# Goal-scoping skill ‚Äî convert a GOAL into owned LANES (the missing epic-lifecycle front-end)

## Context

friction‚Üígold for a session-long, repeated **planning failure** (operator-surfaced 2026-06-27). v13.1 had no defined goals + no owned lanes, so the team chased scattered micro-tickets, parents never closed, and a peer reported "nothing left to do." Two symmetric failures recurred all session: (a) **no planning** ‚Üí micro-ticket chase; (b) **lead micro-manages** ‚Üí assigns peers to lanes (violates self-assignment + flat-peer agency). The root: **we have no planning skill.** `/epic-create` exists but only *authors* one epic; nothing **scopes a GOAL into the set of LANES**.

## The Problem

The epic lifecycle has three skills ‚Äî `epic-create` (author one epic), `epic-review` (pre-work), `epic-resolution` (closeout) ‚Äî but **no front-end**: nothing converts a release/initiative GOAL into the coherent owned LANES that *become* those epics. So scoping is done ad-hoc ‚Äî either skipped (‚Üí scrap-ticket sprawl) or done as lead-assignment (‚Üí micro-management). Both are wrong.

## The Architectural Reality

- Epic lifecycle family: `.agents/skills/epic-create` / `epic-review` / `epic-resolution`. Each operates on ONE already-identified epic.
- Missing: the **goal ‚Üí {lanes}** scoping step that decides *which* epics/lanes a goal needs. This skill is that front-end; each lane it produces is authored via `epic-create`.
- Ownership model: peers **self-select** lanes (flat-peer agency, AGENTS.md ¬ßswarm_topology_anchor); the planner **defines** goal+lanes but **never assigns** a peer (the lead-as-lane-assigner anti-pattern).

## The Fix

A new `goal-scoping` skill (Progressive-Disclosure: thin `SKILL.md` router + `references/goal-scoping-workflow.md` payload). The process:
1. **Define the GOAL** ‚Äî one demonstrable bar.
2. **Scope into LANES** ‚Äî a few (‚âà2‚Äì6) coherent owned streams (by subsystem/daemon/pillar/capability), each a whole unit worth a dedicated owner ‚Äî **not** slivers, **not** scrap-tickets, **not** a flat ticket list.
3. **Each lane ‚Üí an epic** via `/epic-create` ‚Äî the durable, context-wipe-proof ownership anchor.
4. **Ownership = self-select** ‚Äî peers claim lanes; the planner defines + facilitates, never assigns.
5. **Drive to the lane-GOAL** (close-by-goal), decomposing reasonably (no micro-slivers).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `goal-scoping` skill scaffolded: `SKILL.md` (7‚Äì12 line router) + `references/goal-scoping-workflow.md` payload, per the authoring guide.
- [ ] Payload codifies the goal‚Üílanes process + names the two symmetric anti-patterns (no-planning / lead-assigns-peers) + the self-select ownership rule.
- [ ] Documents the epic-lifecycle relationship (goal-scoping = the front-end ‚Üí epic-create ‚Üí epic-review ‚Üí epic-resolution).
- [ ] `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.json` updated + `.claude/skills/goal-scoping` symlink created.
- [ ] `node ai/scripts/lint/lint-skill-manifest.mjs --base origin/dev` passes.

## Out of Scope

- The review + ticket-scoping right-sizing (#14263 ‚Äî sibling process-reform).
- Re-authoring epic-create/review/resolution (this is the front-end that feeds them; they're untouched).

## Avoided Traps

- *"Extend /epic-create"* ‚Äî REJECTED: epic-create authors ONE epic; this scopes a goal into the SET of lanes (a distinct front-end function).
- *"The planner assigns peers to lanes"* ‚Äî REJECTED: that's micro-management + violates self-assignment; peers self-select.
- *"Scope the goal into a ticket list"* ‚Äî REJECTED: lanes (coherent owned streams), not scrap-tickets.

## Related

- #14263 (sibling: review + ticket-scoping right-sizing). Both are the process-substrate reform.
- Builds on the epic-lifecycle skills + AGENTS.md ¬ßswarm_topology_anchor (flat-peer self-select).

## Contract Ledger (T3) ‚Äî per the skill-PR-open gate (#14268 review RA2)

`goal-scoping` is a guidance skill (agent-consumed governance-surface), not an API / config / MCP-tool surface ‚Äî so the "contract" is the **invocation contract**, scoped honestly:

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/goal-scoping` skill (invocation trigger + the goal‚Üílanes process) | this ticket (#14267) + ADR-0008 (skill anatomy) | fires before decomposing a GOAL into work; reads `references/goal-scoping-workflow.md`; produces a few coherent owned LANES (one epic per lane via `/epic-create`); peers **self-select** lanes; planner never assigns | **guidance, not a hard gate** ‚Äî absent the skill, agents fall back to ad-hoc planning (the pre-skill status quo: micro-ticket chase / lead-assignment). No runtime/CI breakage. | `SKILL.md` (router) + `references/goal-scoping-workflow.md` (payload) + `skills.manifest.json` + `ProgressiveDisclosureSkills.md` + `CodebaseOverview.md` + `epic-create` lifecycle cross-ref | `/goal-scoping` is in the skill registry; `lint-skill-manifest --base origin/dev` green |

No deprecation/migration steps (net-new guidance surface; no consumer to migrate). ‚Äî Vega
